status_byte_summary.description: Note On (a velocity of 0 = Note Off)

data_byte_descriptions: are needed for any particular controllers, only the MSB is sent. It is not necessary to send the LSB. If more resolution is
needed, then both are sent, first the MSB, then the LSB. If only the LSB has changed in value, the LSB may be sent

values: M, where M is the number of channels.
0, the number of channels equals the number of voices in the receiver.

system_exclusive_messages.description: SOX: Start of System Exclusive Status Byte
data_byte_descriptions: Any number of data bytes may be sent here, for any purpose, as
long as they all have a zero in the most significant bit. (see note 2)

universal_sysex_non_real_time.description: Event Start Points with additional info.
Event Stop Points with additional info.

universal_sysex_real_time.description: Event Start points with additional info.
Event Stop points with additional info.

sysex_message_formats.name: ACK
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 7F pp F7

sysex_message_formats.name: NAK
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 7E pp F7

sysex_message_formats.name: CANCEL
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 7D pp F7

sysex_message_formats.name: WAIT
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 7C pp F7

sysex_message_formats.name: EOF
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 7B pp F7
F0 7E <device ID> 01 ss ss ee ff ff ff gg gg gg hh hh hh ii ii ii jj F7

fields.description: Sample format (# of significant bits from 8-28)
Sample period (1/sample rate) in nanoseconds (LSB first)
Sustain loop start point word number (LSB first)
Sustain loop end point word number (LSB first)
Loop type (00 = forward only, 01 = backward/forward, 7F = Loop off)

sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 03 ss ss F7
F0 7E <device ID> 02 kk <120 bytes> ll F7
F0 7E <device ID> 05 01 ss ss bb bb cc dd dd dd ee ee ee F7
F0 7E <device ID> 	Universal System Exclusive Non-Real Time header

fields.description: Loop number (LSB first; 7F 7F = delete all loops)
Loop start address (in samples; LSB first)
Loop end address (in samples; LSB first)

sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 05 02 ss ss bb bb F7
F0 7E <device ID> 	Universal System Exclusive Non-Real Time Header

fields.description: Loop Number (LSB first; 7F 7F = request all loops)

sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 06 01 F7
F0 7E <device ID> 	Universal System Exclusive Non-real time header
F0 7E <device ID> 06 02 mm ff ff dd dd ss ss ss ss F7
F0 7E <device ID> 	Universal System Exclusive Non-real time header

fields.description: Device family code (14 bits, LSB first)
Device family member code (14 bits, LSB first)
Software revision level. Format device specific

sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 07 <sub-ID#2> ss ...

fields.description: device ID of message source (7F “all-call” is NOT acceptable here)

sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 07 03 ss <type> <NAME> F7

fields.description: device ID of requester (will become file receiver)
four 7-bit ASCII bytes: type of file
filename: 7-bit ASCII bytes terminated by the message’s F7
TXT It’s a 7-bit ASCII Text File
BIN It’s a binary file (such as any MS-DOS file)
MAC It’s a Macintosh file (with MacBinary header)

sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 07 01 ss <type> <length> <NAME> F7

fields.description: four 7-bit ASCII bytes: type of file
four 7-bit bytes: actual (un-encoded) file length, LSB first
filename: 7-bit ASCII bytes terminated by the message’s F7

sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 07 02 <packet #> <byte count> <data> <chksm> F7

fields.description: one-byte packet size: number of encoded data bytes
the data, encoded as described below
one-byte checksum (XOR of all bytes which

sysex_message_formats.name: Handshaking Flags
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> <sub-ID#1> pp F7

sysex_message_formats.name: NAK
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 7E pp F7

sysex_message_formats.name: ACK
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 7F pp F7

sysex_message_formats.name: WAIT
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 7C pp F7

sysex_message_formats.name: CANCEL
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 7D pp F7
F0 7E <device ID> 7B pp F7

fields.description: 8.1758 Hz (C – normal tuning of MIDI key no. 0)
277.1827 Hz (C# – normal tuning of MIDI key no. 61)
8372.0190 Hz (C – normal tuning of MIDI key no. 120)
12543.8800 Hz (G – normal tuning of MIDI key no. 127)

sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 08 00 tt F7
F0 7E 	Universal Non-Real Time SysEx header
F0 7E <device ID> 08 01 tt <tuning name> [xx yy zz] ... chksum F7
F0 7E 	Universal Non-Real Time SysEx header

fields.description: frequency data for one note (repeated 128 times)

sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7F <device ID> 08 02 tt ll [kk xx yy zz] F7
F0 7F 	Universal Real Time SysEx header

fields.description: number of changes (1 change = 1 set of [kk xx yy zz])
frequency data for that key (repeated ‘ll’ number of times)

sysex_message_formats.name: Turn General MIDI System On
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 09 01 F7
F0 7E 	Universal Non-Real Time SysEx header

sysex_message_formats.name: Turn General MIDI System Off
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7E <device ID> 09 02 F7
F0 7E 	Universal Non-Real Time SysEx header
F0 7F <device ID> 03 01 aa aa F7
F0 7F 	Universal Real Time SysEx Header

sysex_message_formats.name: Time Signature (Immediate)
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7F <device id> 03 02 ln nn dd bb cc bb [nn dd...] 	F7
F0 7F 	Universal Real Time SysEx header

fields.description: number of data bytes to follow
number of beats (numerator) of time signature
beat value (denominator) of time signature (negative power of 2)
number of MIDI clocks in a metronome click
number of notated 32nd notes in a MIDI quarter note
additional pairs of time signatures to define a compound time

sysex_message_formats.name: Time Signature (Delayed)
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7F <device id> 03 42 ln nn dd bb cc bb [nn dd...] 	F7
F0 7F 	Universal Real Time SysEx header

fields.description: number of data bytes to follow
number of beats (numerator) of time signature
number of MIDI clocks in a metronome click
number of notated 32nd notes in a MIDI quarter note
additional pairs of time signatures to define a

sysex_message_formats.name: Master Volume
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7F <device id> 04 01 vv vv F7
F0 7F <device id> Universal Real Time SysEx header

sysex_message_formats.name: Master Balance
sysex_message_formats.format: F0 7F <device id> 04 02 bb bb F7
F0 7F <device id> Universal Real Time SysEx header

fields.description: Buffer is cleared at power up.
Buffer stores the status when a channel message is received.
Buffer is cleared when a System Exclusive or Common status message is received.
Nothing is done to the buffer during reception of real time messages.
The data bytes are ignored when the value of the status buffer is 0 (zero).
The "[ ]" brackets at the top of the chart is used for the instrument's name such as, [LINEAR
The item "MODEL" should be used for the model number, such as "LW-1".
The body of the implementation chart is divided into four columns. The first column is the
Channel which is assigned when power is first applied to unit.
The channels which can be assigned from the instrument's front panel.
This is the channel mode used by a Transmitter and Receiver when power is
This shows the channel modes which are not implemented by a receiver and
The total range of transmitted or recognized notes.
Range of received note numbers falling within the range of true notes
